title: Lemon Japanese Egg Shio Ramen
description: A bright and silky ramen featuring a citrus-infused shio broth, a silky glassy egg, and gentle steam vegetables, perfect for a romantic Kyoto-inspired dinner.
instructions: In a pot, combine water, dashi, soy sauce, mirin, salt, lemon zest, lemon juice, and miso paste; bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let the broth infuse for 5 minutes.
Add sliced shiitake mushrooms and bamboo shoots to the simmering broth.
Bring the broth back to a low simmer and keep warm.
Cook ramen noodles in a separate pot of boiling salted water according to package instructions, about 3 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Place the eggs in a small saucepan and cover with water; bring to a gentle boil and cook for 7 minutes for a soft but firm yolk.
Transfer the eggs to ice water to halt cooking, then peel.
To create the glassy egg, wrap each peeled egg in a thin layer of tempered glass (or a sheet of plastic wrap) and insert it into the hot broth for 1 minute, then remove.
Warm the broth again and remove it from heat.
Divide the cooked noodles into bowls, pour the hot citrus broth over them.
Gently slide the glassy egg into each bowl, then top with warmed mushrooms and bamboo shoots.
Garnish with a sprinkle of green onion and serve immediately.
